response.justification: This candidate is primarily a full-stack web and TypeScript/Node.js developer with no evidence of Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard non-negotiable requirement for this role. Beyond the language disqualifier, the resume shows no experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Linux kernel development, or chip-vendor SDKs. The limited C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, 2014–2015; a bachelor's thesis project) is too dated and shallow to meet the embedded C requirement. The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with every core technical and linguistic requirement of this position.
strengths: Long career with demonstrated breadth across platforms (web, mobile, embedded-adjacent, desktop) showing adaptability
Some low-level C/C++ experience from National Instruments IDE work and a Linux-based audio thesis project
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that transfer to any engineering context
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement and an immediate disqualifier
Zero experience with network security protocols, embedded firmware, LAN switches, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, etc.)
Primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web; C programming is minimal and over a decade stale with no Linux kernel work
